description: Call GET /api/v1/google/trends/search for Google SERP Trends Search through Just Serp API with query.

operations.description: Get Google trends Search data, including interest over time, geo breakdowns, and related queries, for demand analysis and seasonal trend monitoring.

parameters.description: The search term or topic ID to analyze in Google Trends (e.g., 'iPhone', '/m/027lnzs' for Bitcoin). You can provide up to 5 terms separated by commas for comparisons.
parameters.name: query

parameters.description: Set the language for the results using its two-letter code (e.g., 'en' for English, 'fr' for French). See Google Language.
parameters.name: language

parameters.description: The geographic location code to filter trends (e.g., 'US', 'GB'). Omit for worldwide trends. See Google Trends Locations.
parameters.name: geo

parameters.description: Refines results for region charts. Supported values: 'COUNTRY', 'REGION', 'DMA', 'CITY'.
parameters.name: region

parameters.description: The type of trend data to retrieve. Supported values: 'TIMESERIES' (Interest over time), 'GEO_MAP' (Breakdown by region).
parameters.name: data_type

parameters.description: Time zone offset in minutes (e.g., '420' for PDT). Range: -1439 to 1439.
parameters.name: tz

parameters.description: The search category code (e.g., '0' for all categories).
parameters.name: cat

parameters.description: The Google property to filter trends. Supported values: 'images', 'news', 'froogle' (Shopping), 'youtube'.
parameters.name: gprop

parameters.description: Date range filter for the search. Supports predefined values (now 1-H, now 4-H, now 1-d, now 7-d, today 1-m, today 3-m, today 12-m, today 5-y, all) and custom ranges: yyyy-mm-dd yyyy-mm-dd (e.g. 2021-10-15 2022-05-25) or hourly yyyy-mm-ddThh yyyy-mm-ddThh within 1 week (e.g. 2022-05-19T10 2022-05-24T22, based on tz).
parameters.name: date

responses.description: Authentication failed: API Key is invalid or missing
Access denied: Insufficient credits or quota exceeded
Internal server error or upstream service exception
